Title: Vladimir
Nickname: A stain splattered in the pages of history
Age: Current1546 – 2996 years old Timeline Born2000 BN – 550 BN Edit
Family: Alvaro vol Kalah Heigaari, The Lion of Camavor (Distant Nephew) †

children.title: Servant of the Darkin
children.textContent:
Vladimir was once a prince of Camavor, but was given away as a royal hostage to an unknown Darkin as a bargain to save the kingdom from the monster's tyranny. Should the king break faith with the Darkin, the prince would be slain. Vladimir was thus taken far off to where his Darkin master dwelled, but he soon learned that his father had broken his oath within the year. Because his father had sons and heirs aplenty, the death of Vladimir would mean nothing to the king.

Although Vladimir had expected to be killed, the Darkin instead offered Vladimir the chance to lead his armies against his father's kingdom. Vladimir knew the kingdom would never be his, and he thus gladly accepted. Vladimir laid waste to his father's city and he presented his father's head to his master, winning the Darkin's favor and being trusted as one of his loyal vassals. In time, Vladimir came to govern his master's subjects with as little mercy as the Darkin themselves. He eventually believed himself above the Darkin's other mortal servants, and for his devotion, he became the first of the Darkin's servants to be taught the forbidden art of hemomancy. The Darkin sharing their secrets with mortals was punishable by death, but Vladimir's master broke this rule for his own amusement. The Darkin would later teach some of his other followers blood magic, but the practice was remarkably difficult for mortals. For Vladimir's master, hemomancy was as simple as breathing, but Vladimir had to exercise every scrap of intellect and willpower to master the simplest cantrips.

Eventually, after one of the Darkin betrayed their brethren, the god-warriors' hold over the world began to crumble. With his enemies united, the Darkin ordered Vladimir to lead his armies to his defense. But Vladimir had not forgotten the many years of cruelty he had suffered at his master's hands. As suggested in ancient texts written in High Shuriman, Vladimir's master was betrayed by his own warhost, who killed him instead of having him sealed like so many of the others. Secretly, it was Vladimir himself who struck the killing blow, and with the Darkin killed and the measure of his power absorbed, Vladimir had renewed his flesh and extended his lifespan far beyond that of a mere mortal, though his mind suffered as a result of the ordeal. The other mortal servants of the Darkin fled, taking what knowledge they had of blood magic with them. Vladimir continued to extend his life with hemomancy for centuries after this, repeating countless similarly vile rituals to restore his youth at the expense of mortal lives.

Despite leading a war against Camavor and killing his father, Vladimir continued to send the customary, yearly letters back to his home kingdom, addressed to whoever was ruling it at the time. He also dwelled on the Blessed Isles at some point before its destruction. While there, he met a temperamental nature spirit and acquired some rare nightbloom seeds, which he brought with him back to Valoran to be planted in his garden as a reminder of his first life.

children.title: The Fall of Mordekaiser
children.textContent:
During the reign of the Iron Revenant Mordekaiser, it was said that a mythic and bloodthirsty fiend haunted the coastal cliffs of eastern Valoran, demanding young lives and savage worship from the several local barbaric Noxii tribes. Few were welcome in his lair, until the day a pale sorceress approached this barbarian god with an offer. The two feasted together as equals, weaving magic so dark that the wine at their table soured, and the roses withered, vibrant red turning to black. This encounter would result as the formation of the Black Rose, and so began the pact between Vladimir and LeBlanc.

With this alliance, LeBlanc and Vladimir rallied the Noxii barbarians and led the siege against the dreaded tyrant. Together, they neutralized the source of his power, the Immortal Bastion. They managed to sever the anchors of his soul from his armor as well as sealed the empty iron shell away in a secret place, thus Mordekaiser's reign was ended and he was cast out of the material realm. Vladimir and LeBlanc were also able to defeat Mordekaiser's personal hound demons, Atakhan and Tybaulk, whom they sealed away to later be used for their own ends.

children.title: Rune Wars
children.textContent:
Some time after sealing Mordekaiser, Vladimir received word of his distant relative, King Viego of Camavor, whose queen had been poisoned by an assassin's blade. According to legend, Vladimir attempted to contact the king and wrote a letter to him, but the grief-stricken Viego refused to respond, not knowing Vladimir had the power and knowledge that could have saved her. Viego's search for a cure instead led him and his armies to the Blessed Isles, where they unleashed a magical catastrophe known as the Ruination.

With Helia destroyed, the remnants of the ancient order from the Blessed Isles, whose mission had been to gather and protect Runeterra's most dangerous artifacts, struggled to keep knowledge of the World Runes hidden. What followed were years of political tension, as nations were unwilling to trust one another for fear of being unwittingly destroyed by these runes. In the Noxii territories, the leaders of two rival nations met to parley at the village of Khom, where Tyrus of Helia tried and failed to mediate the conflict. The encounter ended in the complete destruction of Khom and the surrounding territories by the power of two World Runes. During the Rune Wars, the last of the Noxii were forced to retreat inside the Immortal Bastion for shelter, where the Black Rose mages shielded the fortress from the magical fallout of the almost apocalyptic battle.

children.title: In the Shadows of the Empire
children.textContent:
When the Noxii finally emerged from hiding, the land beyond had been ravaged but they survived. From that day onward, they were alloyed as a single people, no longer as Noxii but Noxus. The Noxian capital was built in the shadow of the Immortal Bastion's central towers, and from here the Noxian people would strive to expand their holdings in the name of glory. Vladimir and LeBlanc continued their partnership, and he would eventually take up residence in a shuttered, lavish mansion in the Mortoraa, the city's older and wealthier district, where he would live his unnaturally long life as a recluse.

Over the centuries, others joined the Black Rose—powerful nobles, exalted masters of magic, and beings darker still. Rife with disputes, and games of politics and war, Black Rose grew into the hidden power that would guide the throne of Noxus for more than a thousand years, orchestrating many of the empire’s most ambitious campaigns. In three and a half centuries, Noxus became an imperial power after the fall of the Drakkengate, where the noble houses made their pledge to one day unite all of Runeterra under their banner.

children.title: The Chronicles of Vladimir
children.textContent:
As time wore on, Vladimir too grew older, the mortals around him fleeting as he had lived more lives than he was able to count or even recall. Unlike LeBlanc, he was unsatisfied with always keeping in the shadows and would often join the noble courts at intermittent periods in Noxian history, as to ensure he would have a part in major events. Vladimir would spread the art of hemomancy to his various acolytes and students, who would bring its practice into the Noxian military. Yet Vladimir's true nature would remain a closely-guarded secret, and after a few decades he would always fade back into seclusion so none would notice his immortal lifespan, or the atrocities he committed to sustain it.

To preserve the memory of his achievements, Vladimir commissioned artwork for his gallery, each one a reminder of his past glories to be added to his chronicles. Within his mansion there was a defaced sculpture of Mordekaiser, a garden of nightblooms from the Blessed Isles, and a collection of butterflies. Vladimir's paintings were numerous and aged, with the first depicting his leadership of the Darkin warhosts. One depicted him having suffered a grave defeat at the hands of a titanic ivory statue, a battle from which he barely escaped. On occasion, Vladimir would take to painting these portraits himself, sometimes using the tears of orphans to bring imbue them with a perfect melancholy. At the end of each life, Vladimir would retreat from the public eye until he was needed once more by the Black Rose, or when he foresaw another turning point in world affairs.

children.title: Resurrection of Sion
children.textContent: The ruling Grand General of the empire, Boram Darkwill was willing to pay almost any price to restore Noxus' lost glory and made an alliance with the Black Rose. With the help from Vladimir, LeBlanc spearheaded the resurrection ritual to reanimate the long-dead hero Sion, and presented him to the Grand General. Sion, driven mad by unnatural bloodlust and utterly inured to pain, hurled himself like a living battering ram against allies and foes alike, destroying all he faced. The victories Sion brought were costly, and his bloodlust was eventually deemed so uncontrollable that Boram Darkwill ordered for his imprisonment.

children.title: Trifarian Revolution
children.textContent: Toward the end of his reign, Darkwill requested a new kind of weapon be made by the Black Rose hemomancers: a golem who needed no food or water and was driven only by a thirst for blood. The result of this endeavor was Briar, another failed experiment which was deemed too dangerous and had to be shackled in an enchanted pillory to control her. As Jericho Swain launched his coup against Darkwill, the Black Rose sent Briar and the other living weapons to destroy the usurper. However, these experiments were all defeated or captured and Swain escaped unscathed, becoming the new Grand General after Darkwill's death.

children.title: The Silken Danse
children.textContent:
With the unexpected death of Boram Darkwill and the rise of Swain as the new Grand General, the political landscape of the empire changed dramatically, and Vladimir was forced to prepare his eventual return to Noxian politics. He began to secretly attend high-society banquets hosted by his Crimson Circle hemomancers, including Edvin Cortain and the Noradis, but was obvious enough that LeBlanc grew suspicious of his activities. One morning, LeBlanc sent a clone to tail Vladimir as he was lurking through the marketplace for a secret meeting with an unknown individual.

Following the recent failure of her conspiracy in Piltover, LeBlanc was similarly disturbed by the recent events and believed Swain's rise demanded bolder action from the Black Rose. One of her plans involved seeking out an ancient Darkin weapon—a glaive which contained the god-warrior Zaahen—the host of whom would be in attendance at the Silken Danse hosted by Lady Elise, another member of the cabal. LeBlanc had hoped Vladimir would be in attendance and had Elise send him an invite. However, Vladimir did not attend, and the masquerade ball instead received an uninvited guest in Katarina Du Couteau, who attacked and assassinated the Darkin host, despite Elise's efforts to defend him.

children.title: Summoning of Atakhan
children.textContent:
With the arrival of Mel Medarda to their shores, Vladimir approached LeBlanc, suggesting they should turn their attention to the "opportunity" she presented them. To Vladimir's irritation, LeBlanc remained fixated on her hunt for the Darkin. To locate the glaive, she needed information from someone familiar with the spirit realm and decided to use Atakhan, the demonic servant of Mordekaiser which they had sealed a thousand years prior. After completing the ritual, LeBlanc then tricked the demon with a game that bound its will to hers, forcing it to be an unwilling servant to the Black Rose.

After LeBlanc had enslaved the demon, Vladimir arrived just in time to learn where the glaive was being kept, which Atakhan revealed with a dark painting on the wall. Vladimir quickly recognized it as a depiction of the First Lands, which the empire had recently withdrawn from at Swain's command. With this, Vladimir declared LeBlanc's hunt to be fruitless, as she had both no host and no means to convince Swain to approve another invasion. LeBlanc was unswayed by Vladimir's remarks, believing they could retrieve the weapon secretly and without the support of the Noxian warhosts, to which Vladimir reluctantly agreed.

Ultimately, LeBlanc's plan to acquire the Darkin resulted in the death of Atakhan at the hands of Zaahen, who was now free in Runeterra once more. LeBlanc was pleased with what they had just learned: Demons, like the one wielded by Jericho Swain, could be killed. However, Vladimir was more worried about the Darkin, believing the fallen god-warriors might retaliate against him should they learn how he had slain one of their brethren. To learn as much as he could about Zaahen, he requested a re-translation of some fragmented documents unearthed from the old ruins of Koeshin. After careful research, the translator informed Vladimir that Zaahen was not a threat to him, and that they likely shared a common enemy, meaning his efforts to undermine LeBlanc's plan and the host she had been cultivating was all for nothing.

children.title: Commissioning the Portrait
children.textContent: Some time after the proclamation of the Trifarix, Vladimir made plans to return to the public eye as a vocal opponent of the new government. In preparation for this, he commissioned a new portrait from a woman named Maura Betzenia who, while not the most talented among her peers, was a painter who could put her heart and soul onto the canvas. After giving her a tour of his mansion and gallery, Vladimir formed a rapport with Maura and declared his true goals to her: that his ambition was to end his partnership with the Pale Woman and claim the kingdom he had always been denied. Maura accepted Vladimir's offer to join him, and she followed him to the stairs.

children.title: The Daring Darling
children.textContent:
Some years after the invasion of Ionia had ended, Vladimir and one of his sorcerer devotees, Vaquer, went on a mission for the Black Rose to acquire a number of magical artifacts which had been unearthed on the Shadow Isles. The artifacts in question had been brought by treasure hunters for safekeeping in the vault of The Daring Darling inn, located at sea between the Isles and Bilgewater. Vladimir and Vaquer both infiltrated The Daring Darling by checking in, with Vaquer disguising himself as a stuffy Piltovan to spy on the other guests, and Vladimir taking his usual role as a benevolent Noxian aristocrat, secluding himself in his room to avoid attention.

When a Harrowing unexpectedly arrived, Vladimir took the opportunity to strike and began secretly murdering the guests, sowing distrust and turning them against one another. To prevent their escape, Vladimir bound all the ships in chains made from blood. He used the resulting chaos to swipe magical trinkets from the treasure hunters, and after he failed to convince the innkeeper Fayette to open the vault so they might survive the Harrowing, he used one of these amulets—known as Silva's Madstone—to forcibly control her into doing so, after which he stole and adorned almost every artifact in the chamber onto his person. As nearly every guest was dead by this point, Vladimir finally revealed himself as both the saboteur and a hemomancer to Fayette, and also revealed that Vaquer had been his accomplice the entire time.

Vladimir had been impressed by Fayette's knowledge of the Shadow Isles and wanted her to meet with his benefactors. However, one of the guests—Jolera of the Noxian Warmasons—had not yet succumbed to her wounds, and with her last breath she called to Lady Vengeance and cursed Vladimir as her betrayer. Kalista arrived through the Black Mist, and Vladimir, still adorned in magical amulets, reveled in the legendary battle that lay ahead of him. As they fought and destroyed the inn, Vaquer was quickly killed by Kalista, and Fayette fought back against the specters of her guests and managed to escape on a boat using the vault's last remaining artifact: a bell which could disturb the spirit realm and the Black Mist specters. To her dismay, Vladimir also managed to escape onto their boat using the same trick, and he rode a wave of blood to catch up with them. Exhausted, wounded, and having lost all his new artifacts in the battle, Vladimir agreed not to fight the innkeeper and instead requested she take him to Bilgewater.

mainContent.title: Appearance
mainContent.textContent: Vladimir is a tall man with an eternally youthful face. He has medium-length white hair and dark crimson eyes that appear blue as they originally were while he is in public, and glow when he utilizes his magic. He generally wears lavish, aristocratic clothing, often black and red with a silver or gold trim. His signature outfit consists of knee-high boots, vertically striped pants, and a slim overcoat with a tall, regal collar, ornate patterns on the front, and coattails which extend to his boots. Notably, he wears long metal claws on his fingers that resemble talons, from which he is able to twirl glowing spheres of blood magic at will.

mainContent.title: Personality
mainContent.textContent:
Vladimir is an ambitious being that desires to fulfill his immortal life with the pleasures of the world. His flamboyant behavior hides his cruelty and sadism, that indulges when using his powers on others.

Vladimir made himself a home in Mortoraa, a big castle in a small district within the Immortal Bastion, known for being one of the wealthiest neighborhoods in Noxus Prime. Unlike many other districts which are typically cramped, the streets in Mortoraa are luxurious and comfortably spacious. However, at night it has a reputation as the most ominous district for the streets are never illuminated with torch-light or lanterns.

Uniquely among all other members of the Black Rose, Vladimir has rarely limited himself to scheming from the shadows. He is very sociable. He prefers to hanging around between the public eye and the secret societies that he built. In the past, he deigned to join the Noxian noble courts during the most interesting of times, only to fade into seclusion decades later. History has lost track of Vladimir on many occasions, though its pages are littered with figures suspected to have been him.

He is aware that his memory would not be able to keep up with his extremely old age. Most of his unnaturally extended life endures not in his memory, but in his chronicles. There were a select few memories that Vladimir refused to forget, and so he labored to do what his mind could not. He wrote books about himself. To remember them, he draws and paints portraits that tell the story that is mostly the turning point of event in the world. Portraits of himself were hung at the circular walls in his castle. His portraits shows him at a very different times in his life, and all portraits have different framing or style. Those brief moments their lives touched, what felt like an eternity ago. More melancholy, but fitting perhaps. To Vladimir, there was nothing in the world as beautiful as sadness.

children.title: LeBlanc
children.textContent:
Vladimir and LeBlanc first made their pact during the dark reign of Mordekaiser. Despite not usually taking guests, Vladimir allowed her into his lair where they feasted as equals and made a deal that would break the Iron Revenant's hold over Valoran and seal him away for good. Together with the Noxii barbarians, Vladimir and LeBlanc achieved what no one else could during that era, and they seized the dark magical secrets hidden away within the Immortal Bastion for themselves.

As the leaders of the Black Rose, LeBlanc and Vladimir have schemed for centuries together. They consider each other equals in strength, as two immortal mages who have manipulated the course of Noxus since its foundation. Yet centuries of power games, disputes, and war could only lead to distrust, and neither LeBlanc nor Vladimir are under any illusion about the other's betrayal. LeBlanc believes Vladimir is an arrogant fool for his attendance of social events, too enamored with his own glory and not nearly as secretive as he believes himself to be. Likewise, Vladimir has been critical of LeBlanc's management of the Black Rose, believing her leadership has only driven them further into chaos, notably with her recent scheme to unleash the Darkin, which he saw as reckless and tried to sabotage. The two undermine each other frequently behind closed doors, and Vladimir has already killed numerous of LeBlanc's clones who have gotten too close trying to spy on him. LeBlanc also finds his practice of hemomancy somewhat revolting, but has been unable to outlive him due to his immortality.

children.title: Black Rose
children.textContent:
Vladimir is one of the founders of the organization alongside LeBlanc. He has aided the Black Rose for centuries, with its hemomancers responsible for the resurrection of Sion, the creation of Briar, and he has even personally participated in wars for the empire against Demacia, as suggested in his old portraits. Yet Vladimir cares little for the other members or machinations of the Black Rose, only assisting them when it suits his own ends. Vladimir is also not above sabotaging the Rose if he decides the plan does not serve him. And unlike the cabal's more secretive members, he has often stood out among others and prefers to show up in public from time to time, attending social events and making political waves in the Noxian noble courts.

As a result of his venturing into the public eye, Vladimir was able to create his own cult—the Crimson Circle, and under his tutelage it was able to find a place in the military of Noxus. LeBlanc herself as a low opinion of the Vladimir's hemomancers and creations, but the rapid growth of his influence has been cause for concern among the Rose, with his ambition making him a potential rival to their schemes.

children.title: Darkin
children.textContent:
When ancient Camavor was threatened during the Great Darkin War, the king gave one of his unneeded heirs to the monstrous god-warrior as proof of his loyalty. This prince, Vladimir, would later become one of the Darkin's most prominent mortal followers, leading wars and commanding subjects, and even being taught the god-warriors' most well-kept secrets, such as the forbidden art of hemomancy. Like most Darkin, however, Vladimir's master was cruel, and while at first believing the Darkin saw a spark of greatness within him, he soon came to realize he was nothing more than a pet to the Darkin—like a dog on a leash, or a monkey being taught to dance. Vladimir grew to resent his master, and when the Darkin's enemies had united, Vladimir killed him, stealing a measure of his power and extending his life beyond mortal limits.

As one of the few on Runeterra capable of killing a Darkin, and having lived through the Darkin War himself, Vladimir is glad to see them gone and hopes to never encounter one again. When LeBlanc was seeking out Zaahen, Vladimir deliberately sabotaged her plan for fear the god-warrior would learn what he had done and retaliate against him, though there exists no evidence the other Darkin know who Vladimir is nor care enough to try and kill him.

children.title: Jericho Swain
children.textContent: Vladimir has recently brought himself back into the public eye, becoming a vocal opponent against the Trifarix, and this has caught Swain's attention. However, Vladimir's previous public identity has not been completely forgotten, and it is hinted that Swain may know of Vladimir's true nature. One day, a group of soldiers from the Trifarian Legion attempted to intercept the elderly Vladimir on his way home, and they were all killed and absorbed by Vladimir's hemomancy as a part of his plan to revitalize himself. The incident was witnessed by one of Swain's ravens, which Vladimir made eye contact with before it flew away.

children.title: Sion
children.textContent: During the reign of Grand General Boram Darkwill, the hemomancers of the Black Rose resurrected the long-dead Noxian war hero Sion as an undead juggernaut, driven by an uncontrollable bloodlust. Having been cursed with no peace of death, Sion only remembers fragments of his old life, but in moments of lucidity he is able to recognize Vladimir as the one who did this to him.

children.title: Galio
children.textContent: In one of the portraits in his gallery, Vladimir was depicted in a great battle against a titanic statue of ivory stone, having been bloodily wounded in the aftermath. While the specific battle Vladimir participated in is unknown, the Noxian defeat at the scene was evocative of one of the great petricite golems of House Durand—possibly Galio himself, who crushed the Noxian warmages at the Battle of the Greenfangs.

children.title: Maokai
children.textContent: The nightblooms grown in Vladimir's mansion were taken from a grove on the Blessed Isles before their destruction, making the flower nearly extinct. The temperamental nature spirit tending this grove was possibly Maokai, who at present is far more bothered by the nightbloom's virtual extinction than Vladimir appears to be.

children.title: Viego
children.textContent: Although Viego calls Vladimir his uncle, Vladimir was born centuries earlier and thus they are only distantly related due to descending from the royalty of Camavor. After Isolde was poisoned, legend has it that the grief-stricken Viego refused to respond to Vladimir's letter, not knowing Vladimir had the power that could have saved her.

children.title: Kalista
children.textContent: Vladimir was soul-marked by Kalista after his name was invoked by one of the guests he betrayed in The Daring Darling inn. However, he escaped and was able to banish her with the help of a magical artifact. Kalista is related to Vladimir as well, both drawing their lineage from the royal family of Camavor.

children.title: Briar
children.textContent: At the request of Boram Darkwill, Briar was created by the Black Rose and its many hemomancers, a group that primarily serves Vladimir. While it is unknown if he had a direct hand in her creation, Briar somewhat considers Vladimir her 'dad', though she questions whether or not to actually call him that.

mainContent.textContent:
The following change log is for the Character blurb found at the top of the article:

For historical versions of other stories, please see each story's respective change log.
